text.title: GCP Security and IAM Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `GCP Security and IAM`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production projects MUST NOT assign primitive roles (`roles/owner`, `roles/editor`, `roles/viewer`); predefined or custom roles only, scoped to resource > project > folder.

rationale.source: GCP IAM best-practices, IAM Recommender docs

rule.rationale: Primitive roles grant hundreds of permissions; predefined roles + Recommender keep blast-radius small.

rule.statement: CI/CD pipelines MUST authenticate via Workload Identity Federation; long-lived service-account JSON keys are forbidden in repos and CI secrets.
rule.rationale: Static keys are the dominant GCP credential-leak vector (Mandiant 2024); WIF removes the key surface entirely.

rule.statement: Cloud Storage / BigQuery buckets holding production data MUST sit inside a VPC Service Controls perimeter with explicit ingress + egress policies.
rule.rationale: IAM alone cannot stop a compromised identity exfiltrating data to an external project; VPC-SC enforces network-layer perimeter.

rule.statement: Sensitive resources (Cloud SQL, GCS buckets, Persistent Disks) MUST use CMEK with automatic key rotation period ≤ 90 days.
rule.rationale: Google-managed encryption is opaque; CMEK gives the org a kill-switch and rotation cadence required by SOC2 / ISO27001.

rule.statement: Admin Activity, Data Access, and System Event audit logs MUST be routed to an immutable BigQuery sink in a separate project with retention ≥ 400 days.

rationale.source: GCP audit logging docs, CIS GCP Foundations 2.x

rule.rationale: On-project audit logs can be tampered by Owner; cross-project sink provides forensic durability.

rule.statement: If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology; document skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
